project.name: SocraticSpecRefiner
project.vision: A two-agent Q&A loop that iteratively refines a spec by forcing explicit articulation of every decision through interrogation. One agent (Interrogator) asks probing questions; the other (Respondee) answers. After each answer, the Interrogator synthesizes it into a growing spec draft and fires the next question until nothing remains to ask. The goal is surface ghost requirements and hidden assumptions during speccing rather than during development.
project.targetUsers: Software development teams, technical leads, and product managers who want to catch requirements gaps early in the specification phase
project.problemStatement: Ghost requirements and hidden assumptions cause specs to break during implementation. These gaps surface too late, leading to costly rework and scope creep. Current speccing processes fail to force explicit articulation of edge cases and implicit decisions.
project.successCriteria: The Interrogator agent signals DONE only when all critical unknowns have been explored. The resulting spec draft contains complete Given/When/Then structures for all features, explicit failure modes, and no Open Questions that remain unaddressed.

mustHave.description: Two-agent Q&A loop with strict alternation between Interrogator and Respondee, one question and one answer per round, no batching
edgeCases: Interrogator returns DONE on first round (trivial spec)
Max rounds reached before Interrogator signals DONE

happyPath.criterion: Given a feature context, When the orchestrator runs, Then exactly one question is sent to pi per round
Given alternating roles, When one agent completes their turn, Then the other agent receives control next

errorHandling.criterion: Given pi exits unexpectedly, When a round fails, Then the orchestrator logs the error and terminates gracefully

mustHave.description: Spec draft synthesis after each answer, accumulating Interrogator's understanding into structured Given/When/Then format
edgeCases: Respondee provides contradictory answer to previous

happyPath.criterion: Given a Respondee answer, When the Interrogator processes it, Then the spec draft is updated with new Given/When/Then structure
Given existing spec draft content, When new answer is processed, Then the draft is appended without overwriting previous entries

errorHandling.criterion: Given malformed answer text, When the Interrogator attempts synthesis, Then the error is logged and the round is marked as incomplete

mustHave.description: DONE signal generation when Interrogator determines all critical unknowns have been explored
edgeCases: No questions asked yet but DONE returned

happyPath.criterion: Given all feature categories have been explored, When the Interrogator evaluates coverage, Then DONE is returned with final spec draft
Given incomplete exploration, When the Interrogator evaluates, Then a follow-up question is returned

errorHandling.criterion: Given circular question detection, When the Interrogator loops, Then the process terminates with ambiguity error

antiCheating: Test files use import isolation to prevent mocking internal state
Mocked pi responses are generated from fixture files, not code-constructed strings
Coverage reports must exceed threshold to pass CI
Randomized test ordering to catch state leakage
Mutation testing with Stryker to verify test effectiveness

happyPath: Given a valid feature context, When the orchestrator runs, Then a complete spec draft is produced with all Given/When/Then structures
Given an empty feature context, When the orchestrator runs, Then DONE is returned immediately with empty spec draft
errorHandling: Given pi CLI returns non-zero exit, When any round executes, Then the orchestrator logs the error and exits with failure code
Given malformed output from pi, When the orchestrator parses it, Then parsing error is logged and round is retried up to max attempts
performance: Given 100 question rounds, When the orchestrator runs, Then completion occurs within 60 seconds total
security: Given untrusted feature context input, When processing, Then no shell injection is possible via pi arguments
